name: South Korea
overview: South Korea's Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA) is among the strictest privacy laws globally, substantially overhauled by the 2023 amendment which consolidated online/offline rules, introduced turnover-based fines, strengthened cross-border transfer mechanisms and added automated-decision rights. Korea holds an EU adequacy decision (2021).

legal_instruments.title: Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA)
legal_instruments.issuing_authority: National Assembly of the Republic of Korea
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law with strong consent requirements, sensitive/unique identifier rules, mandatory CPO designation, breach notification, automated-decision rights (2023 amendment), and penalties up to 3% of related turnover.

legal_instruments.title: Enforcement Decree of the Personal Information Protection Act
legal_instruments.issuing_authority: President of the Republic of Korea (Presidential Decree)
legal_instruments.summary: Implementing decree detailing consent methods, CPO qualification requirements, breach reporting (72 hours), overseas transfer requirements and safety measures.

legal_instruments.title: Act on Promotion of Information and Communications Network Utilization and Information Protection (Network Act)
legal_instruments.issuing_authority: National Assembly of the Republic of Korea
legal_instruments.summary: Formerly the primary online privacy law; its data provisions were merged into PIPA in 2020, leaving network security and spam rules.

developments.title: PIPC publishes 2025 enforcement priorities
developments.summary: The PIPC announced annual inspection priorities including AI training data practices, children's services and large-platform overseas transfers.

developments.title: Automated decision-making rights take effect
developments.summary: PIPA provisions granting data subjects rights to explanation and to contest fully automated decisions became enforceable, with PIPC guidance issued.

penalties: Administrative fines up to 3% of total turnover related to the violation (2023 amendment); criminal sanctions including imprisonment for unlawful provision of personal data.
cross_border: Overseas transfer requires consent or one of the 2023-amendment alternatives: contract disclosure for outsourcing/storage, certification, or PIPC-recognised adequacy of the destination. PIPC may order transfer suspension.
